ogTitle: Tom Groggin vs Tonghi Creek: Climate Safety

heading: Tom Groggin vs Tonghi Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tom Groggin, Victoria and Tonghi Creek, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tom Groggin has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Tonghi Creek (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tom Groggin or Tonghi Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tom Groggin has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Tonghi Creek scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tom Groggin scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Tonghi Creek scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tom Groggin or Tonghi Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tom Groggin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
